kattukāraka.meaning: the relation to the action as agent; being an agent
kattukāraka.summary: A grammatical term: kattukāraka is the agent relation, paired with kammakāraka, the relation to the action as patient.

katvāpi.meaning: even having done; having done also
katvāpi.gram: gerund of karoti + enclitic particle api, fused by sandhi
breakdown: Even, also. (The final -ā elides the a-: katvā + api → katvāpi.)

kayirā.meaning: one could do; one should do
kayirā.gram: verb, 3rd person singular optative of karoti (irregular: kareyya > kayirā)
breakdown: Optative ending; the irregular metathesis gives kayirā.

kañci.gram: indefinite pronoun, masculine accusative singular (kaṁ + ci)

kaḷevarassa.meaning: of the body; of the corpse

kaṭukattāya.gram: neuter abstract noun (dative/instrumental form of kaṭukatta)

kaṭṭha.summary: In the source it appears in a list: dry grass, wood and cow dung (tiṇa-kaṭṭha-goma).
breakdown: Wood, a piece of wood, firewood.

keci.gram: indefinite pronoun, masculine nominative plural (ke + ci)

kevalakappaṁ.meaning: the whole of it; entirely, completely
breakdown: Here as a suffix-like member: "about, virtually, in its entirety".

kevalaparipuṇṇaṁ.meaning: complete and perfectly full; entire in every way
kevalaparipuṇṇaṁ.summary: Part of the stock description of the Dhamma well proclaimed: complete and pure in its entirety.

khamanīyaṁ.summary: Used in the polite inquiry "is it bearable?" when asking after someone's health.
khamanīyaṁ.gram: potential participle of khamati (neuter nominative/accusative singular)
breakdown: Root: to endure, to bear, to forgive (khamati).
Potential participle suffix: "that can be endured".
Leaping, springing; the lexicon cites it in the compound khandana-lakkhaṇa, "having leaping as its characteristic mark".
khandana.note: uncertain: the lexicon's gloss line is fragmentary ("with + aspiration, leaping + characteristic mark"); the parse follows that fragment.

khandhānaṁ.meaning: of the aggregates; of the five sensorial aggregates
breakdown: Aggregate, mass, heap: the five khandhas (rūpa, vedanā, saññā, saṅkhāra, viññāṇa) that make up the whole mind-matter structure.

khantimettānuddayasampanno.meaning: endowed with tolerance, loving kindness and compassion

khantisoracce.meaning: tolerance and gentleness; forbearance and sympathy

khantī.goenka: Khanti, tolerance or forbearance, is counted among the ten pāramīs that a meditator develops on the path: sīla, nekkhamma, viriya, khanti, sacca, adhiṭṭhāna, paññā, upekhā, mettā and dāna. In practice it shows itself as the capacity to bear hardship and provocation with a calm and equanimous mind, without generating new reactions of aversion.

breakdown: Sound, voice. (The doubling ss is junction doubling.)

khayaṁ.gram: masculine noun (accusative singular of khaya, from khīyati)
breakdown: Root: to be exhausted, to waste away (khīyati); strengthened to khay-.

khayāya.meaning: for the extinction; for the destruction (of something)
breakdown: Dative singular ending: "for the sake of".

khañjo.gram: adjective (masculine nominative singular, from khañjati "to limp")

khaṇḍampi.meaning: even a broken piece; even a fragment
khaṇḍampi.gram: masculine noun khaṇḍa (accusative singular) + enclitic particle api
breakdown: Even, also. (khaṇḍaṁ + api → khaṇḍampi by sandhi.)

khaṇḍiccaṁ.meaning: the state of being broken; brokenness (of teeth, in the description of aging)
breakdown: Abstract-noun suffix: khaṇḍicca, the state of being broken.

khemangato.meaning: having attained the state of security; arrived at safety
breakdown: Gone to, attained (past participle of gacchati).

khemato.meaning: as safe; as full of peace
khemato.gram: adjective khema with the ablative-adverbial suffix -to: "from the standpoint of safety"

breakdown: Secure, safe; often of nibbāna as the secure place free from danger.

khemino.gram: adjective (khemī, from khema; here nominative plural)

khettavatthupaṭiggahaṇā.meaning: accepting fields and land; the acceptance of property
khettavatthupaṭiggahaṇā.gram: neuter action noun, compound (here ablative: "from accepting fields and land")

khippamantaradhāpesi.gram: adverb + verb, aorist 3rd person singular causative (khippaṁ + antaradhāpesi)
breakdown: Caused to disappear (aorist causative of antaradhāyati "to vanish").

khitto.meaning: cast, thrown; discharged; upset (as in cittaṁ khipati, an upset mind)
khitto.gram: past participle of khipati (masculine nominative singular)

breakdown: I am (1st person singular of atthi). (kho + asmi → khosmi.)

khuramuṇḍaṁ.meaning: closely shaven with a razor; razor-shaved (head)

khuṁsenti.gram: verb, 3rd person plural present of khuṁseti

khvassa.meaning: indeed... of this; because of this
breakdown: Indeed (emphatic particle); before a vowel kho becomes khv-.
Of this, of him (genitive/dative singular pronoun).
Indeed (emphatic particle); before a vowel kho becomes khv-.

khvettha.meaning: indeed here; indeed in this case
breakdown: Indeed (emphatic particle); before a vowel kho becomes khv-.

khāyati.meaning: to appear; to be known, be seen
khāyati.gram: verb, 3rd person singular present, passive of √khā

khāṇukaṇṭakaṭṭhānaṁ.meaning: places of stumps and thorns; thorny ground with tree stumps
breakdown: Place. (The doubling ṭṭh is junction doubling.)

khīradāpanādikaṁ.meaning: beginning with causing milk to be given (to the newborn), and the like
breakdown: Causing to give (causative action noun from dadāti).

khīrodakībhūtā.meaning: blended like milk and water; become as milk mixed with water
khīrodakībhūtā.summary: A simile for living in harmony: as inseparable as milk blended with water.

khīṇa.summary: As in khīṇabhavasaṁyojanā, "the fetters of becoming completely destroyed".
breakdown: Root: to be exhausted, to waste away (khīyati).

khīṇabījā.meaning: with the seed destroyed; whose seed (of future becoming) is destroyed
breakdown: Seed; figuratively the seed of future existence.
